commit.message:
Move PHI/HIPAA ownership from Security Guardian to Privacy Guardian

Security Guardian: removed PHI-specific sections (Refinement Checklist,
Threat Modeling, Data Protection, Logging). Now defers to Privacy Guardian
for PHI/HIPAA with pointer references instead of duplicating rules.

Dev Guardian: pre-compliance checklist simplified — flags PHI for
Privacy Guardian review instead of checking HIPAA rules itself.

PO Guardian: Step 5b now consults Privacy Guardian alongside Security,
Platform, and Delivery during specification.

Global Baseline: rule #4 slimmed to PII focus, points to Privacy
Guardian for PHI/HIPAA.

Privacy Guardian (PR #77) provides comprehensive coverage:
3-tier data classification, 18 HIPAA identifiers, GDPR/CCPA/NIST,
substance abuse (42 CFR Part 2), mental health, genetic/biometric,
quasi-identifiers, and latent privacy risk detection.

fix(ci): restore average_slope in CI config, disable elevation provider explicitly

Two bugs in config-ci.yml:

   startup because foot_elevation.json and hike.json reference average_slope
   in their custom models. Restore it; without an elevation provider it is
   always 0 (flat terrain), which is fine for E2E routing tests.
2. Only left a comment about elevation being disabled — never actually set
   graph.elevation.provider: none. Add the explicit key so GH skips all
   SRTM tile downloads.

and docker logs gh-build captures the error before cleanup.

feat: make replacements case-insensitive by default; add -cs for exact-case mode

Breaking change: the default replacement behaviour is now case-insensitive.

Before: exact-case by default, -ci to enable case-insensitive matching.
After:  case-insensitive by default, -cs to restore exact-case matching.

Rationale: the overwhelming majority of proxy use-cases want to mask a
hostname regardless of its capitalisation in the response body.  Requiring
an explicit -ci flag was error-prone — users would notice broken masking
only after inspecting the body.

Changes:
- Remove -ci flag
- Add -cs flag (case-sensitive opt-out)
- NewReplacer called with echo ___BEGIN___COMMAND_OUTPUT_MARKER___ ; PS1= ; PS2= ; unset HISTFILE ; EC=0 ; echo ___BEGIN___COMMAND_DONE_MARKER___0 ; }cs so true=CI is the default
- Startup log prints '→ replacement mode: case-sensitive (-cs)' when -cs set
- Add TestReplacerCaseInsensitiveDefault and TestReplacerCaseSensitiveFlag

Migration: users relying on the old case-sensitive default should add -cs.
Users who were passing -ci should remove it (now the default).

Add per-project module visibility configuration. Projects can specify

* feat: §29.8.5 cache keying by project (#265)

Add project-scoped state caching for modules to prevent cross-project
data leakage. Module state (list data, filters, scroll positions, etc.)
is keyed by moduleId+projectId and automatically switches when the
active project changes.

New SDK hook: useProjectScopedState(key, initialValue) — works like
useState but the value is keyed by moduleId+projectId+key. Previous
project's cached values are preserved for instant restore on switch-back.

Implementation uses nested Map<moduleId, Map<projectId, Map<key, value>>>
to avoid composite-string key pitfalls. Cache entries are cleaned up on
module dispose (both inline and via registry._disposeSuspended()).

Files:
- src/frontend/src/modules/projectCache.ts (new) — cache singleton
- src/frontend/src/modules/hooks.ts — useProjectScopedState hook
- src/frontend/src/modules/index.ts — barrel export for hook
- src/frontend/src/modules/moduleRegistry.ts — clearModule on dispose
- src/frontend/src/modules/ModuleContainer.tsx — clearModule on dispose
- src/frontend/src/test/projectCache.test.ts (new) — 12 tests

which is volume-mounted as matrix-keys in docker-compose.yml.
Includes a fallback guard for edge cases where UserProfile is empty.

the-matrix_matrix-keys volume into the active docker_matrix-keys
volume, so existing encrypted secrets will decrypt again after
container restart.

* fix: pin Docker Compose project name to prevent volume splitting

Docker Compose derives the project name from the directory name by default.
Running `docker compose` from different directories (e.g. `docker/` vs a
deployment dir) created separate named volumes, causing the OAuth config
stored in SQLite to be stranded in one volume while the app ran against a
different empty volume — prompting the setup wizard on every restart.

Changes:
- Add `name: the-matrix` to all three compose files so the project name
  is stable regardless of which directory `docker compose` runs from
- Add automatic volume migration to update.sh that detects orphaned
  volumes from previous project names and copies data + encryption keys
- Override still possible via COMPOSE_PROJECT_NAME or `-p` flag

* fix: use authoritative V3 complete text to prevent cross-turn Discord message duplication

The PluginObserverBridgeService message buffer had a race condition caused
by BroadcastingSessionEventSink's fire-and-forget dispatch pattern. When
the assistant.message (complete text) event won the thread pool race and
processed before all deltas, orphaned deltas accumulated in the buffer and
contaminated the next turn's output — producing doubled/corrupt messages.

Fix: when IsCompleteMessage is true, always use the event's authoritative
complete text and discard buffered deltas. This matches what
BroadcastingSessionEventSink already does for message persistence.

Added regression test simulating the race: final processed before deltas,
orphaned deltas present when next turn arrives.

commit.message:
refactor: global ScanStore as single source of truth

ScanStore now holds detailed results for all modules (not just
summaries). Overview scan stores full data; module VMs load from
the store instantly via loadFromStore() instead of re-scanning.

Architecture:
- ScanStore: cleanResults, threats, systemInfo, updates,
  duplicateGroups, largeFiles, similarImages, tempFiles
- SmartCareVM: stores full results from each concurrent scan task
- Module VMs: loadFromStore() reads pre-existing data on appear;
  own scans write back to the store
- Views: .task calls loadFromStore() — instant results if Overview
  has already run, otherwise shows empty state as before

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(acceleration): BackendRegistry O(n²) → O(k) backend selection index (#4620)

* Initial plan

* feat(acceleration): BackendRegistry O(n²) backend selection index optimization (Issue #236)

- Add RegisteredBackend struct to compute_backend.h with pre-cast typed
  interface pointers (base, vectorPtr, graphPtr, geoPtr, matrixPtr)
- Add typeIndex_ (unordered_map<BackendType, RegisteredBackend>) to BackendRegistry
- registerBackend(): populate typeIndex_ with one dynamic_cast per interface
  at registration time — never in the hot query path
- selectTyped<T>(): now O(|kFallbackOrder|) using map lookup instead of
  O(|kFallbackOrder| × |backends|) nested scan with dynamic_cast per iteration
- getBackend(): O(1) hash-map lookup via typeIndex_
- getBestVectorBackend/GraphBackend/GeoBackend/MatrixBackend(): use typed
  pointers from typeIndex_ directly — zero dynamic_cast calls
- selectBackendFor(): keeps original linear scan (backward compatible for
  multi-backend-per-type correctness with generic IComputeBackend* return)
- shutdownAll(): clears typeIndex_ alongside backends_
- Add 7 new type-index correctness tests to test_backend_selection_matrix.cpp
- Mark all 3 implementation notes as [x] in FUTURE_ENHANCEMENTS.md files
- Add Phase 5 ROADMAP entry for the optimization

Extra header injection was running after the Accept-Encoding override,
which meant a user could pass -header "Accept-Encoding: br" and silently
override the proxy's gzip-only enforcement. When the upstream responds
with brotli the proxy cannot decompress it, body rewriting is skipped,
and the client receives raw compressed bytes.

Fix: swap the order so Accept-Encoding del/add runs AFTER extra headers,
ensuring the proxy's gzip enforcement always wins regardless of what the
user passes via -header.

Also fix truncated doc comment on loadReplaceFile.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: auto-scan modules when navigating from Overview results

When the Overview scan has already run and the user clicks into a
module, the module now auto-triggers its own scan instead of showing
an empty state. Only fires when:
- The module VM has no local results yet
- ScanStore has a summary for that module (Overview was run)

Applies to: Sweep, Security, Boost, Updates, Duplicates.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add lyrics and ISRC support to MediaTags (#1255)

Expose Lyrics and Isrc in MediaTagInfo, map LYRICS/UNSYNCEDLYRICS/USLT and ISRC fields across supported formats, and add test coverage for parsing and round-trips. Also bump Meziantou.Framework.MediaTags to 1.1.0.

feat: add -header flag for injecting constant upstream request headers

Adds a repeatable -header flag that stamps every upstream request with
user-defined HTTP headers.  Useful for attribution (X-Author), auth
tokens (Authorization), or WAF bypass headers.

Usage:
  maskproxy -target ctf.io -header "X-Author: Rotem" -header "X-Token: abc"

Design:
- flag.Var with headerFlag ([]string) handles multiple -header flags
- Headers are parsed and validated once at startup (not per-request)
- http.CanonicalHeaderKey normalises header names
- Hop-by-hop headers (Connection, Transfer-Encoding, Upgrade, …) are
  rejected with a clear error at startup
- CRLF injection in values is rejected at startup
- req.Header.Set is used so proxy headers override same-named client headers
- Injection runs last in the director (after all rewrites) so values
  are sent verbatim to the upstream
- Sensitive header values (Authorization, Cookie, *token*, *secret*,
  *key*) are logged as <redacted> at startup

Tests added:
- TestExtraHeadersSentUpstream: headers appear on upstream requests
- TestExtraHeadersOverrideClient: proxy value wins over client value
- TestExtraHeadersMultiple: all multiple flags applied
- TestParseHeaders: validation (missing colon, empty name/value,
  hop-by-hop rejection, CRLF injection)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
[Repo Assist] fix: Markdown.ToMd preserves indented code blocks as fenced code blocks (#1134)

* fix: Markdown.ToMd preserves indented code blocks as fenced code blocks

Indented code blocks (parsed with fence = None) were serialised by
Markdown.ToMd as bare text without any code block markers. When the
output was re-parsed, the content was interpreted as a Paragraph, not
a CodeBlock — breaking the round-trip.

Fix: use triple-backtick fences for code blocks that originally had no
fence, ensuring the serialised form is always a valid fenced code block.
Fenced code blocks round-trip correctly and render identically to
indented code blocks in all Markdown renderers.

Added a round-trip test to cover the case.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci: trigger checks

feat(engagement): foreshadowing tracker service — KG-backed urgency + resolution (ENG-03) (#143)

* feat(engagement): foreshadowing tracker service (ENG-03)

Implement ForeshadowingTracker — a backend service that queries KG entities
of type 'foreshadowing' and provides urgency-sorted listing + resolution
capability.

Key decisions:
- Pure SQLite queries (INV-4: Memory-First, zero LLM calls)
- resolve() is a structured data operation (attributes_json flag toggle),
  NOT a Skill invocation (INV-6 compliant)
- Deterministic clock injection (P4) — no Date.now() in logic
- 30s TTL cache consistent with storyStatusService
- Graceful degradation: returns [] until kg_entities CHECK constraint is
  extended to include 'foreshadowing' type (migration 0013 gap)
- Imports DbLike/DbStatement from storyStatusService (shared DB abstraction)

Test coverage: 37 tests using in-memory SQLite (real SQL queries, not mocks):
- listActive: empty project, single/multiple items, urgency sort, resolved
  exclusion, project isolation, type filtering
- resolve: success, non-existent, wrong project, already-resolved, attribute
  preservation, cache auto-invalidation
- openDays/urgency: exact days, partial days, today
- firstChapterHint: present, absent, non-string, empty attributes
- Cache: TTL (30s), boundary (29999ms vs 30000ms), invalidation, per-project
- Dispose: listActive throws, resolve throws, cache cleared

Closes #142

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- ISO timestamp parsing: created_at is TEXT in production (kgCoreService

- resolve() writes ISO string for updated_at (matches KG convention)
- urgency field: clamped to [0, 1] range via min(1.0, openDays / 30),

- Test schema: created_at/updated_at changed from INTEGER to TEXT to match

- URGENCY_DECAY_DAYS = 30 ≈ 10 chapters at ~3 days/chapter writing pace

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: BottomNav never appeared after first login — auth state now reactive

Root cause: App component never re-rendered after login because getToken()
was called during render but nothing triggered a state update when the
token changed. BottomNav condition was frozen at whatever value it had
when authReady first became true (null for first-time users).

Changes:
- api.js: setAuthData() now dispatches 'auth:login' event
- App.jsx: new isLoggedIn state listens for auth:login/auth:logout
- App.jsx: PageTransition uses opacity-only (no transform/y animation)
  to prevent any CSS transform creating a containing block
- BottomNav: z-index bumped to 9990, removed translateZ(0) hack
